summary_one_line: Input must contain a three-part X.Y.Z qualified column reference (non-NULL zDb) that fails to resolve, reaching the db-qualified error path.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A SQL statement whose column reference is a three-part schema-qualified name (X.Y.Z, e.g. db.table.column) so that lookupName is called with a non-NULL zDb; the name must fail to resolve so cnt!=1 and control reaches the error block, where 'if( zDb )' (98156) is then true.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seed carries repeated dotted multi-part qualifier structure (e.g. '.112/1111', 'E.E.E' runs), and the d=2 dispatch shows the winner taking case TK_DOT (L98355 T=1) which builds zDb/zTable/zColumn before calling lookupName; at d=1 the winner takes 'if( zDb )' true (L97837 T=1) and the blocker 'if( zDb )' true (L98156 T=1).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are flat single-token or repeated-letter payloads ('SELECT bbLLLL...', 'SELECT bbbbb') with no nested X.Y dotted qualifier; the loser never builds zDb (d=1 L97837 T=0 F=20, blocker L98156 T=0 F=20) — it routes through the unqualified column path (case TK_ID, L98354 T=20).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: The pair delta is value_profile: both fuzzers share the same input-to-state comparison-substitution mutator, but only value_profile_cmplog adds the CMP_MAP gradient (Hamming/prefix-distance buckets). Reaching the db-qualified branch requires the input to first survive several near-miss string comparisons (sqlite3StrICmp on db names, schema-name matching) and to emit a syntactically valid dotted reference; the CMP_MAP gradient rewards incremental Hamming progress toward those multi-byte comparison targets, letting vpc assemble the X.Y.Z structure that plain cmplog (edge-bucket feedback only) cannot incrementally climb toward.

evidence_trail.claim: The decisive pair is value_profile_cmplog beating cmplog with the technique delta being value_profile.
The blocker is the true branch of 'if( zDb )' inside the error block of lookupName.
Taking the blocker true side requires zDb non-NULL, and the loser never satisfies it.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: L98156  T=1 F=0  T=0 F=20  if( zDb ){  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The winner reaches the dotted-name dispatch (TK_DOT) that constructs zDb/zTable, while the loser stays on TK_ID.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: L98355  T=1 F=7  T=0 F=573  case TK_DOT: {

evidence_trail.claim: The winner-resolving seed contains repeated dotted multi-part qualifier structure (E.E.E runs).
Loser-blocking seeds are flat repeated-letter payloads with no nested dotted qualifier.
value_profile_cmplog adds the CMP_MAP gradient on top of cmplog's I2S buffer.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
union of cmplog and value_profile — edge counters,
per-execution CMP buffer (`CmpLogObserver`), and CMP_MAP gradient buckets.

evidence_trail.claim: Reaching the schema-qualified path passes through string-compare gates on database names (sqlite3StrICmp).

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is CMP_MAP gradient (the pair delta is value_profile: vpc beats cmplog and both already carry I2SRandReplace, so the differentiator is value_profile's Hamming/prefix-distance CMP_MAP buckets, not I2S). Lineage dash-row verification is mandatory only for I2SRandReplace claims and is not applicable here; the CMP_MAP gradient leaves no dash-row signature in seed metadata.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating an error path behind a multi-part dotted-name comparison shows value_profile_cmplog and cmplog reaching the gate at statistically indistinguishable rates (no value_profile advantage), indicating the CMP_MAP gradient is not what assembles the X.Y.Z structure.

weakest_evidence_point: Only one winner-resolving seed is shown, so the inference that the value_profile gradient (rather than lucky havoc) is what assembled its dotted E.E.E structure rests on a single example plus the per-trial prob_div.
